steps.text: Gather information for internal premeeting to prepare for the Tribal meeting.
Identify the Tribal contacts and their roles within the Tribal communities.
Discuss with the Tribal liaison whether the meeting is privileged or confidential.
Identify issues and topics for discussion and review agency information.
Determine if the meeting is high level or staff level.
Identify the desired outcomes for the meeting.
Document the meeting as publicly available unless indicated otherwise by the Tribal government.
Clarify with the Tribal government if the planned meeting is public or nonpublic.
Identify meeting participants including NRC staff and Tribal representatives.
Identify the appropriate rank of the NRC representative for the meeting.
Identify meeting participants who could include NRC staff, Tribal technical representatives, the NRC senior staff, or the Tribal head or Tribal Council.
Identify the appropriate rank of the NRC representative given the activity involved, the level of interaction, and the established agenda.
Consider using various meeting formats, including presentations followed by discussions, listening sessions, small 'breakout' groups, and question and answer sessions (or a combination of formats).
Provide an opportunity for the Tribe to present a proposed meeting agenda in advance when planning meetings with Tribal participants.
Share the NRC’s proposed agenda with the Tribe.
Include suggestions from both the NRC and Tribal agendas in the final agenda.
Contact the Office of the General Counsel if a Tribal representative makes a statement pertaining to or asserting.
Dress respectfully as for any business meeting with elected officials or distinguished representatives of a government.
Wear professional, business attire at meetings held in Washington, DC and at NRC Headquarters.
Consider casual attire for meetings held outside the metropolitan area or in informal settings.
Determine the need to provide explanations for technical and licensing terms.
Respectfully listen and ask questions for clarification.
Communicate with the Tribe early and often.
Familiarize yourself with the Tribe’s specific information and current issues.
Hold face-to-face meetings to get to know the Tribe and its technical resource staff and council representatives.
ESTABLISH FAMILIARITY AND CONTINUITY because they are essential elements for establishing and maintaining effective relationships.
KEEP THE FOCUS WHERE IT BELONGS because Tribes and their concerns should be highlighted during introductions and the meeting table.
BE ADAPTABLE by exhibiting flexibility, sensitivity, and empathy in Tribal relations.
BE PATIENT. Take time to understand, communicate with, and work with the Tribes who have an interest in NRC activities.
